alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google Devices and Services scaling global commercial operations and transformation programs; experience with customer-facing transformation and scaling
alignments.job_requirement: Work directly with enterprise customers to understand strategic priorities and operational challenges, identifying where AI can deliver measurable business value

alignments.candidate_evidence: Scaling global operations and running transformation programs implies stakeholder management and cross-functional coordination
alignments.job_requirement: Facilitate workshops, hackathons, and innovation sessions with customers to explore new ways AI can transform their operations

alignments.candidate_evidence: Global commercial operations and transformation program leadership suggests executive communication and stakeholder management experience
alignments.job_requirement: Demonstrate strong presentation skills to both internal and external stakeholders, including executives

alignments.candidate_evidence: Product operations and strategy background with transformation program ownership; no explicit supply chain, finance, or domain-specific case evidence provided
alignments.job_requirement: Have a good understanding of business processes across sectors (such as Supply Chain or Finance) with the ability to translate high-level business needs into specific AI use cases

gaps.gap: Native-level French language proficiency explicitly required for customer workshops, executive presentations, and stakeholder management. Candidate shows professional English and native German, but French proficiency not indicated.
gaps.mitigation: If candidate has native or near-native French, this is immediately resolvable; otherwise, requires language acquisition or role relocation outside Paris/French-speaking client base.

gaps.gap: 5+ years in solutions consulting, data/AI consulting, technical pre-sales, sales engineering, or customer-facing solution architecture. Candidate background is product operations and strategy at Google, not consulting or pre-sales verticals.
gaps.mitigation: Consulting-style delivery, client engagement models, and technical pre-sales credibility differ from product operations; significant onboarding and skill transfer required.

gaps.gap: Some experience prototyping solutions with Python and modern AI frameworks. Candidate notes Python is basic and actively learning; no production AI/ML prototyping or GenAI application build experience evident.
gaps.mitigation: Rapid upskilling on Python, LangChain, RAG, and AI orchestration frameworks; reliance on engineering partners for prototyping depth.

gaps.gap: Generative AI, LLM orchestration (LangChain, LlamaIndex), and modern AI architecture familiarity. Not mentioned in candidate profile.
gaps.mitigation: Self-directed learning and mentorship from Celonis AI/ML team; customer-facing role allows co-learning with partners like OpenAI, Microsoft, Databricks.

gaps.gap: Industry domain expertise in supply chain, finance, or operations required to build credibility with strategic customers. Candidate background is Google consumer/device operations, not enterprise supply chain or finance.
gaps.mitigation: Domain expertise can be developed through customer engagement and mentorship; Google operations experience provides adjacent foundation.

hard_blockers.evidence: "Native-level or near-native French language skills (written and spoken), with the ability to confidently engage French-speaking customers, facilitate workshops, and present to executive stakeholders." Role location is Paris, France, and French is explicitly required for customer-facing workshops and executive engagement. No French proficiency indicated in candidate profile.

output.summary: Candidate's eight-year Strategy and Operations background at Google aligns with Value Engineering's customer-facing transformation and stakeholder-management scope, placing her at an appropriate senior IC/manager level. However, the role is a poor fit: it requires 5+ years in solutions consulting, technical pre-sales, or AI consulting—verticals distinct from product operations—plus Python prototyping and GenAI/LLM expertise that the candidate is still developing. Most critically, native-level French is a hard requirement for Paris-based customer workshops and executive presentations; no French fluency is indicated. The combination of language barrier, lack of consulting/pre-sales background, and limited AI/Python depth makes this a skip despite strong operational leadership pedigree.
uncertainties: French language proficiency not stated in candidate profile; if candidate is fluent, this removes the hard blocker
Depth of customer engagement and pre-sales experience at Google unclear; may have done more consulting-style work than profile suggests
Supply chain and finance domain familiarity at Google not specified; may have relevant context
AI/GenAI learning trajectory and pace not detailed; time-to-productivity on Python and LLM frameworks unknown
